subject: Mathematics
title: Islamic Geometric Patterns
description: A fast breakdown of Islamic Geometric Patterns for builders.

hook.text: Medieval Islamic artists created geometric patterns of breathtaking complexity using just a ruler and compass.

buildup.text: Their designs feature interlocking stars, hexagons, and polygons arranged with perfect symmetry.

discovery.text: These patterns encode all 17 possible wallpaper symmetry groups—a classification mathematicians didn't complete until 1891.

twist.text: Patterns at the Alhambra in Spain even display quasicrystalline symmetry—500 years before Penrose.

climax.text: Islamic geometric art is one of the greatest mathematical achievements of the medieval world.

punchline.text: Artists discovered mathematical symmetry centuries before mathematicians.

quiz.question: How many distinct wallpaper symmetry groups exist?
